message:
refactor: harden convergence verdict and apply idiomatic Rust patterns across progress_certificate and latency_algebra

progress_certificate.rs:
- Extract evidence-building into dedicated build_evidence() method,
  shrinking verdict() from one monolithic block into two focused
  functions. The verdict method now computes metrics and delegates
  auditable evidence assembly.
- Tighten convergence gate: add violation_rate ceiling (25%) and
  an empirical reduction_ratio check as an alternative to the
  Azuma signal, so that traces with strong downward trend but
  noisy Azuma bounds can still be classified as converging.
- Replace if-let-else chains with map_or / then() for
  estimated_remaining and effective_step_bound selection.
- Use mul_add() for fused multiply-add precision in
  expected_remaining calculation.
- Switch format strings to captured-identifier style
  (e.g. {est:.1} instead of {:.1}, est).
- Fix tests calling verdict.increase_count() → cert.increase_count()
  (the method lives on the certificate, not the verdict).
- Add explicit oscillation-rejects-convergence assertion.
- Tighten azuma_bound_decreases_with_more_steps test by setting
  max_step_bound to match actual step size (10.0) for a tight bound.
- Add clippy allow attributes on test module for cast_lossless,
  cast_precision_loss, and suboptimal_flops.

latency_algebra.rs:
- Replace match-on-Option with map_or_else throughout the analyzer
  (analyze_node, analyze_leaf, analyze_join min/max rate selection,
  analyze_race winner selection).
- Use f64::from(u32) instead of as-casts for lossless int→float
  conversions in sample generation loops.
- Use f64::midpoint(lo, hi) instead of manual (lo + hi) / 2.0 in
  binary search for guaranteed midpoint correctness.
- Use mul_add for fused multiply-add in segment evaluation, curve
  construction, and t_max calculation.
- Replace Self-referencing type names with Self in ServiceCurve
  methods (sequential, scale).
- Reformat long lines and simplify test assertions (remove
  unnecessary multi-line assert! calls, use approx_eq instead of
  assert_eq for floating point comparisons).
- Rename shadowed test variables for clarity
  (join_ab → lhs_join_with_b, etc.).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
